text: The surgeon begins adhesiolysis, applying traction and counter-traction to identify dissection planes. Cold scissors are used to carefully separate adhesions, revealing white lines that indicate clear dissection planes, while sparing the peritoneum and avoiding thermal damage.

The surgeon visualizes the uterus and injects vasopressin into the myelodendron, using a dilution of twenty units in one hundred ml or forty units in two hundred ml, to aid in adhesiolysis and reduce blood loss.
